prompt:
Phase 3 two-arm trial for adjuvant renal cell carcinoma. 3:1 (experimental vs. control) enrollment ramp. 
Primary: DFS, follows an exponential distribution.
Gated secondary: OS, follows an exponential distribution.
Single analysis each, both tested for one-sided. Overall two-sided alpha = 0.05.  
DFS is tested with 250 events, and OS is tested with 430 events. 
HR: 0.70 for DFS, 0.75 for OS
5-year survival rate in control: 80%
5-year DFS rate in control: 60%
Two endpoints are independent. 
N = 830.
expected_output:
One output file with

- Rate or median of DFS/OS in both arms
- Efficacy boundaries (at least nominal p-values or HR boundaries at each look)
- Multiplicity diagram
- Power to claim efficacy on DFS and OS
- Trial duration
assertions: Use correct allocation in two arms
Rates for exponential distribution are correctly derived from 5-year event rates for both endpoints
DFS is tested at one-sided 0.025, OS is tested at one-sided 0.025 only if DFS is significant
No alpha split — DFS uses full 0.025, not a fraction
OS events derived independently with Schoenfeld at full 0.025
No graphical multiplicity node for OS (zero initial alpha)
Report the probability of claiming efficacy on DFS
Report the probability of claiming efficacy on OS (defined as both DFS and OS are significant)
